description: This figure presents multimodal electron microscopy analysis of microstructural deformation in Fe–10Al–15Mn–0.8C–5Ni wt% low-density steel, comparing cold-rolled (CR) conditions and sample A15 (conventionally processed and annealed at 900°C for 15 min) after interrupted tensile testing at plastic strain e = 0.05. Panels (a) and (b) show EBSD phase and Kernel Average Misorientation (KAM) maps of the CR condition, revealing co-deformation of B2 stringer bands and austenite (γ) with strain localization predominantly in the softer γ phase. Panel (c) provides a TEM bright-field image of A15, confirming plastic deformation in both γ and B2 with higher dislocation density in γ. Panels (d) and (e) display high-resolution EBSD phase and KAM maps of A15, visualizing geometrically necessary dislocation (GND) localization at γ/B2 interfaces and γ grain boundaries. The images demonstrate strain partitioning between the hard B2 intermetallic and soft γ matrix, where dislocations localize at phase interfaces, reducing GND density in the bulk γ (64% volume fraction) and contributing to yield point elongation and reduced strain hardening exponents (0.19–0.27).
